# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const targetUrl = url.searchParams.get("url");

  if (!targetUrl) {
    return new Response("URL parameter is required", { status: 400 });
  }

  const permission = await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" });
  if (permission.state !== "granted") {
    return new Response("Network permission not granted", { status: 403 });
  }

  try {
    const response = await fetch(targetUrl);
    const data = await response.text();
    return new Response(data, { status: 200, headers: { "Content-Type": "text/plain" } });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response("Error fetching the data: " + error.message, { status: 500 });
  }
}

addEventListener("fetch", (event) => event.respondWith(handler(event.request)));
